description_long: This sculpture depicts two stylized human figures, both kneeling back-to-back on a rectangular plinth. Each figure has their arms raised and hands clasped behind their heads, creating a mirrored, symmetrical composition. The forms are robust and simplified, with smooth, rounded contours and minimal surface detailing, emphasizing the figures' musculature and posture. The sculpture is rendered in a light gray stone, giving it a monolithic and timeless appearance. The base is a solid rectangular block, providing stability and a sense of permanence. The overall impression is one of strength, unity, and contemplative stillness.
description: A stone sculpture of two kneeling human figures back-to-back with arms raised, rendered in smooth, simplified forms on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures kneeling back-to-back, with their arms raised and hands clasped behind their heads, highlighting the symmetry and muscular forms.
description_view_1: Side view focusing on the left figure's face and torso, with the right figure mostly obscured, emphasizing the forward gaze and posture of the left figure.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of both figures, their arms forming an arch above, and the details of their backs and legs visible.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the right figure's face and torso, with the left figure mostly obscured, mirroring the composition of view 1.
attribute_subject_matter: two kneeling human figures in a mirrored pose
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the sculpture is isolated against a white background.
